description: Trigger discrimination: pre-work per-lane knowledge transfer vs end-of-work closeout/handoff (ai-closeout-reporter), whole-lifecycle contract (ai-sdlc-operating-model), standing tool-instruction alignment (agent-instruction-consolidator), and the lane's approval-scope grant it cites (scoped-approval-register).

cases.prompt: Before the frontend agent starts its lane of the multi-agent effort, produce its guide: the request lifecycle it sits in, the contracts it must honor, the recipe and checklist per route, and exactly what it must not touch.
cases.reason: Evidence-cited planner-to-implementer transfer BEFORE a lane's work begins is the lane guide — not a terminal report, not the lifecycle-stage contract, and not tool-instruction file alignment.

cases.prompt: The backend lane just completed its last handler — report what changed, what was intentionally not done, validation results, and the handoff notes for the next stage.
cases.reason: Work's END: disclosure of what actually happened with evidence is the closeout; the lane guide was work's BEGINNING.

cases.prompt: Define how our humans and agents work together across the whole lifecycle — stages, gates, and who holds authority at each stage.
cases.reason: The end-to-end stage/authority contract is the operating model; a lane guide is one lane's knowledge packet inside such a contract.

cases.prompt: Our CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and Cursor rules disagree about branch naming and test commands — align them into one source of truth.
cases.reason: Standing tool-instruction alignment across assistants is the consolidator; lane guides are per-effort, per-lane knowledge, not standing instruction files.

cases.prompt: Record that the AI-routing lane is approved to modify src/ai/router only, nothing else, per today's sign-off.
cases.reason: Recording the lane's authorization grant with allowed/forbidden scope is the register; the lane guide cites that entry, it does not create it.
